reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading is factually correct but misunderstands library guarantees. The c-kzg-4844 v2.1.1 library implements the cryptographically-specified `compute_cells` function which MUST return exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB (128) cells by design per EIP-4844/PeerDAS specification. This is not a runtime validation issue but a fundamental cryptographic property - the library would have to violate the specification entirely to return ≠128 cells. Spec reference (fulu-validator/SG-002) confirms: cells are defined as Vector[Cell, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB], a fixed-size vector. The library trust is appropriate here - if c-kzg-4844 returns incorrect cell counts, the entire KZG proof system is broken, not just this code path. No evidence of such a bug exists in v2.1.1. The CellsAndProofs struct construction with mismatched lengths would be caught downstream if this hypothetical bug existed, but the premise (library bug) is speculative without evidence. Severity: N/A (by-design library behavior, not a vulnerability).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: fulu-validator/SG-002 (cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction) defines cells as Vector[Cell, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB] - fixed-size vector of exactly 128 cells per blob, guaranteed by cryptographic specification
